title: CUET UG 2026 — Common University Entrance Test
meta_description: CUET UG 2026: 200+ universities (Delhi, JNU, BHU). May 11–31, 2026. Section-wise MCQs on NCERT. Delhi DU, JNU minimum cutoffs.

hero.one_liner: Single entrance exam for 200+ universities including Delhi University, JNU, BHU, Jamia Millia, and 40+ National Law Universities

what_is_it: CUET UG (Common University Entrance Test) introduced in 2022 replaced merit-based admissions (12th marks) at Central Universities (Delhi University, JNU, BHU, etc.) with a standardized entrance exam. Conducted by NTA (National Testing Agency) in computer-based format across multiple sessions. Before CUET, Delhi University had 99%+ cutoffs in board marks — creating unfair scenario where only top 0.5% students could enter. CUET democratized access: 12th board marks are now secondary; entrance exam score determines merit. With 15+ lakh applicants and 200+ participating universities, CUET is among India's largest entrance exams.

CUET UG structure: Section IA/IB (Languages — 13 options), Section II (Domain Subjects — choose up to 6 from 27), Section III (General Test). Each section has MCQs with questions based on NCERT Level 12 standards — conceptual clarity matters, not memorization. For language: grammar, comprehension, vocabulary from 12th NCERT books. For domain subjects (like Economics, History, Geography): stick to NCERT — beyond NCERT is rarely asked. General Test covers: General Knowledge, Current Affairs, Logical Reasoning, Quantitative Reasoning. Unlike CUET, there's no essay/descriptive component.

Universities set their own CUET cut-offs. Delhi University top colleges (SRCC, St. Stephen's, Hindu, LSR) require 99–99.5 percentile. JNU requires 95+ percentile. BHU requires 90+ percentile. For engineering: IIT-based universities (Delhi, BHU have engineering) have higher cutoffs than others. For law: top NLUs (NLSIU, NALSAR) through CLAT separate; CUET applies to law programs at general universities. Merit list preparation: Each university weighs CUET score + board marks + interview (if applicable) differently — check each university's admission policy.

key_details.label: Eligibility
key_details.value: 12th pass from any recognized board (appearing also eligible)

key_details.label: Section Structure
key_details.value: Section IA/IB (Languages) + Section II (Domain, choose 6) + Section III (General)

key_details.label: Question Type
key_details.value: MCQs (40 per subject attempted, out of 50 offered)

key_details.label: Marking
key_details.value: +5 for correct, -1 for wrong, 0 for unattempted

exam_pattern.description: Attempt max 6 domain subjects. Each section 45 min. Choose sections per university requirements.

subjects.name: Section II: Domain Subjects (choose up to 6 from 27)

extra_sections.heading: Top Universities: CUET Cutoff Ranges (2025 Data)
CUET Strategy: Subject Selection Guide
content: Step 1: Target Universities & Programs — Before CUET registration, shortlist 5–7 universities with their specific programs. DU: SRCC (commerce), St.
Stephen's (versatile), Hindu (sciences), Ramakrishna Mission (all streams). JNU: All programs competitive.
BHU: Engineering + humanities. Jamia: Engineering + humanities.
Step 2: Check Exact Subject Requirements — Critical step many students miss. DU's SRCC requires: English (mandatory) + Economics, Accounting, Business Studies (you need 3 of 4).
BHU Engineering requires: Physics, Chemistry, Math, Language. JNU has flexibility — most programs accept interdisciplinary subject combinations.
Check official admission notices on university websites.

Step 3: Strategic Subject Selection — Choose 6 subjects that: (1) You're strong in (aim for 95%+ in each), (2) Satisfy requirements of your top 3 universities, (3) Minimize overlap (reduce prep load).

Example strategy for commerce-legal studies path: English (mandatory, top colleges need it), Economics (commerce + law), Political Science (law + social sciences), History (liberal arts, JNU), Accounting (commerce), Business Studies (commerce).
This 6-subject combo covers: DU commerce, JNU multi-disciplinary, Jamia law.
Stream-wise Combinations: Commerce: English + Economics + Accounting + Business Studies + Math/Statistics + Computer Science (flexibility in last 2).
Science: English + Physics + Chemistry + Biology + Math + Computer Science (all 6 usually required).
Humanities: English + History + Geography + Political Science + Economics + Psychology/Sociology (flexibility in final choices).
Interdisciplinary (trending): English + Economics + Computer Science + Psychology + Environmental Studies + Media Studies (attractive for research-oriented universities like JNU).

preparation_tips: NCERT 12th textbooks are your core material — read them thoroughly. CUET doesn't go beyond NCERT in difficulty. If you know 12th NCERT well, CUET will be straightforward. Solve all textbook exercises.
Domain subjects are 60% of CUET score (6 × 200 = 1,200 marks out of 1,900 total). Focus 70% of preparation on domain subjects. General Test is 250 marks — important but secondary.

Current affairs: 30–40 questions in General Test from current affairs (2024–2026). Read The Hindu or Indian Express for 30 min daily, 6 months before exam. Make short notes on major events, policy changes, international news.

Practice official NTA mock tests — CUET interface and question pattern are specific. Taking mocks helps you familiarize with: negative marking impact, time management (45 min per section), subject-wise strategy. Take 10–15 full-length mocks by June 2026.

faqs.question: Is CUET mandatory for all Central Universities?
faqs.answer: Yes. Since 2022, CUET is mandatory for all Central University undergraduate admissions (except deemed universities). 12th board marks alone don't determine admission. However, board marks are still considered (20–40% weight) alongside CUET score and interview (if applicable).

faqs.question: How to manage 6 subjects + General Test in CUET?
faqs.answer: Total sections: 3–4 (Language 1 mandatory + optional Language 2 + Domain subjects + General Test). You don't attempt all on same day — CUET is held across multiple days/sessions (May 11–31). Check admit card for your allotted day and session. Each session covers specific sections.

faqs.question: If I score 90 percentile, can I get Delhi University?
faqs.answer: Possibly, but depends on your subject scores, board marks (20–30% weight), and specific college cutoff. SRCC (commerce) at DU requires 99+ percentile — 90 percentile won't suffice. But other DU colleges (like Ramakrishna Mission) might accept 90. Check each college's specific cutoff.

faqs.question: Can I apply to multiple universities with one CUET score?
faqs.answer: Yes! CUET score is valid across all 200+ participating universities. You take CUET once; then apply separately to each university's counselling/admission portal. Each university conducts its own merit list based on CUET score + board marks + interview (if applicable).

syllabus.subject: Section IA: Language (13 languages)
syllabus.topics: Reading comprehension, vocabulary, grammar, sentence correction, rearrangement. Choose any language from 13 options.

syllabus.subject: Section II: Domain Subjects
syllabus.topics: Choose up to 6 subjects from 27 options. Questions based on NCERT 12th syllabus of that subject. Popular: Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Maths, Economics, Accountancy, History, Political Science.

syllabus.subject: Section III: General Test
syllabus.topics: General knowledge, current affairs, quantitative reasoning, logical reasoning, numerical ability, general mental ability

study_plan.tasks: CUET is 100% NCERT-based for domain subjects. Read NCERT 12th textbooks thoroughly for your chosen subjects. Make chapter-wise notes. Solve NCERT back exercises. Language section — practice comprehension and grammar.
Solve CUET previous year papers (2022-2025). General test preparation — current affairs, basic maths, reasoning. Practice MCQs from Arihant/Disha CUET guide. Focus on your top 3 domain subjects.
Full mock tests weekly. Time management — each section is 45-60 minutes. Focus on accuracy since there's negative marking. Revise NCERT notes. Target: 180+ per section for top central universities.

quiz.question: CUET UG is mandatory for admission to:
How many domain subjects can you choose in CUET?
